items.title: Belle Burden’s Strangers Is Sparking a Financial Revolution
items.description: Belle Burden’s hit memoir about how she nearly lost control of her fortune during her divorce is pushing women across the US to take a harder look at their financial lives. Bloomberg's Abha Bhattarai

items.title: South Africa’s Absa Faces Deeper Slide as Analysts Turn Cautious
items.description: A selloff in Absa Group Ltd. shares may have further to run as the South African lender’s disappointing earnings guidance causes once-bullish analysts to turn cautious on the stock.

items.title: Pfizer Vaccine Dispute Hits Polish, Romania Air Traffic Agencies
items.description: Poland and Romania’s legal battles with Pfizer Inc. over unused Covid-19 vaccine purchases are spilling into the funding of the eastern European nations’ air traffic agencies.

items.title: Europe’s Earnings Momentum Is Taking Off, and That’s Unusual
items.description: After two years of almost uninterrupted downgrades, analysts are now raising European earnings estimates at the fastest pace since mid-2024.

items.title: Leveraged Bets on SK Hynix to Multiply After US Debut
items.description: ETF issuers have at least a half dozen products ready to launch next week.

items.title: Spain’s Cuerpo Touts US Business Ties to Diffuse Trump Tensions
items.description: Spain is striving to strengthen business links with the US, despite tensions with Donald Trump, according to Economy and Trade Minister Carlos Cuerpo.

items.title: Apollo Approaches Record Spend With EasyJet and Bayer Deals
items.description: Apollo Global Management Inc. is on course for a record year of acquisitions at a time when many of its private equity peers are struggling to deploy capital.

items.title: Cencosud Shares Tumble as Strategic Plan Yet to Boost Earnings
items.description: More than a year into a new strategic plan at Chile’s Cencosud SA, the retailer’s shares have slumped and the company is asking investors for patience.

items.title: BMW, Volkswagen Sales Decline on Worsening Slump in China
items.description: BMW AG and Volkswagen AG’s vehicle sales fell in the second quarter due to a worsening decline in China, where mounting competition from domestic rivals and a property crisis is stifling demand.

items.title: Nigeria Take Steps to Win Frontier Market Status as Stocks Surge
items.description: Nigeria is taking steps to ensure that its red-hot stock market wins a status upgrade by an influential index provider, as the West African nation seeks to capitalize on renewed investor interest.

items.title: Iran War Forces Japan to Rethink Its Love For Plastic
items.description:
Japan loves plastic. But the war in Iran is forcing it to rethink its love for the cheap material and the feasibility of individually wrapping every product.

Alice French explains. (Source: Bloomberg
